L1.area: User account management and profile request handling

L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where user profile input fields lack maximum length validation in their request validation rules. An attacker can submit excessively large string values in user profile fields via crafted HTTP requests, potentially causing integer overflow in string length processing and leading to a Denial of Service (DoS) condition.

L3.area: User profile creation and update request validation within the CMS user management package
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists where user profile input fields such as name, phone, and account identifiers lack maximum length validation in the user creation and update request validators. An attacker can submit excessively large string values via crafted HTTP requests, potentially causing integer overflow in string length processing and leading to a Denial of Service (DoS) condition.

ground_truth.reason: The user creation and update request validators do not enforce maximum length constraints on profile input fields, allowing attackers to submit excessively large values that cause integer overflow in string processing, leading to Denial of Service.
